title: Trigger
description: A Trigger is an event that initiates an operation — either from a user, an external system, or the passage of time. Triggers are the entry point to all behavior.

operation.description: The operation this trigger initiates, expressed as Resource.Action.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what initiates this trigger.
examples: Fires when a borrower submits a loan application form.
Fires nightly to default any loans past their due date.

schedule.description: A cron expression defining when the trigger fires. Required when source is 'schedule'.

event.description: The webhook event name that fires this trigger. Required when source is 'webhook'.

after.description: The operation whose completion fires this trigger. Required when source is 'operation'.

condition.description: An optional condition that must be true at the time of the trigger for the operation to be initiated.

examples.description: Fires when a borrower submits a loan application form.
Fires when the payment processor sends a payment.completed webhook.
Fires nightly to default any active loans past their due date.
Fires automatically after an order is fulfilled.
